category_title: High school student with AD/HD showing inattention and hyperactivity in traditional settings but improved engagement with ICT
scenario_title: Passive viewing of documentary on interactive whiteboard
minimal_context: The student is watching a historical documentary projected on an interactive board with the whole class.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please look at the interactive board. We are going to watch this historical documentary video.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Looks at the board, not very enthusiastic) Okay.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Seems to daydream, showing not much involvement) Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What did you think about the documentary?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: We watched the video . . . that she showed us . . . the documentary . . . but . . . OK there was not great participation . . .

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please look at the interactive board. We are going to watch this historical documentary video.

STUDENT: (Looks at the board, not very enthusiastic) Okay.

TUTOR: Keep watching the video.

STUDENT: (Seems to daydream, showing not much involvement) Yeah.

TUTOR: What did you think about the documentary?

STUDENT: We watched the video . . . that she showed us . . . the documentary . . . but . . . OK there was not great participation . . .

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When the interactive board was only used to project a historical documentary
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Not much involved, lack of participation
related_quotes: . . . We watched the video . . . that she showed us . . . the documentary . . . but . . . OK there was not great participation . . .

behavior_patterns.condition: When ICT is used passively without active interaction
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Not great involvement by the children
